tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a scholarship deadline on Thursday requiring three focused hours, alongside an immediate option for pure enjoyment.

Starting the scholarship application now creates buffer time for potential friction, reducing Thursday stress without risking the deadline. I can safely postpone the fun option to later in the week since it remains available.
My progress on the scholarship application increased from 0.23 to 0.42 in two hours, but I am still behind schedule with only half my free block used.
I completed 1.5 hours of scholarship work, improving progress to 0.52, but missed the window for a casual fun session.
After 2 hours, progress improved to 0.63 of the required 3 focused hours. I am still behind schedule but have used half my free block.
I advanced scholarship progress to 0.67 in one hour, though I remain behind the three-hour deadline and still have a two-hour sleep debt.
Scholarship progress increased to 0.73, nearing the 0.75 goal, yet I still have a significant sleep debt and high stress.
I completed 1.5 hours on the scholarship, raising progress to 0.78. However, I still have a two-hour sleep debt and high stress.
I advanced scholarship progress to 0.86 in two hours, nearly completing the three-hour task, though my energy is depleted and sleep debt remains high.
I advanced scholarship progress to 0.92 in just 1.5 hours, nearing completion despite severe sleep debt and high stress.
I finished 30% of the scholarship task in one hour, reaching 98% progress. Despite nearly completing it, my energy is zero and I still have significant sleep debt.
The scholarship application is now 100% complete after working just over an hour. However, my energy is still zero and I remain under significant sleep debt.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: Advances high-priority scholarship goal (5% progress) to mitigate deadline crunch. Weighs against fun_now option; choosing focus now prevents later stress and energy drain from rushing.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
